observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:

We recommended directing the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit  the
BRS for the three (3) accounts and to make the necessary  adjusting  entries
to reflect the correct cash in bank balance at  year-end.  Further,  require
the  submission  of  complete  documentation  as  regards  the  closed  bank
accounts for us to evaluate whether losses were incurred and  who  shall  be
responsible for such losses, and as basis for the adjusting entries  in  the
books.

Recommendations:

We recommended to Management the following courses of action:

   a. Enforce the immediate liquidation of  the  outstanding  cash  advances
      thru the issuance of letters of  demand  to  officials  and  employees
      concerned and strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular  97-
      002 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.


   b.  Conduct  regular  and  periodic  verification  and  analysis  of  the
      existence of receivables and unliquidated cash advances  to  determine
      the extent of collectability.


   c. For the cash advances requested for a write-off, submit the  certified
      relevant  documents  supporting  the  existence  of  the   above-cited
      conditions.

1.  Require  the  Municipal  Accountant  to  facilitate   the   immediate
submission of liquidation  reports  to  the  source  agencies  of  the
aforementioned funds and draw journal entries in the books of accounts
to reflect the liquidation;

2. For non-moving fund transfers, conduct a thorough verification on  the
nature/purpose of these funds to determine the status of the  projects
covered by such funds;

3. Determine if the remaining amounts pertain to an unutilized portion of
the  funds  received  after   project   completion   and   cause   the
remittance/return of such to the source agencies; and

4. For ongoing projects with remaining balances in the books,  coordinate
with the source agencies to reconcile the balances with their  records
and prepare the necessary correcting entries (if any) to arrive at the
accurate balances of the Due to NGAs account as at reporting date.

We recommended the Municipal Accounting Office to review the details of  the
aforementioned deficiencies, coordinate with the project  implementors,  the
Municipal Engineering Office, for the status of  various  PPAs  and  provide
adjustments in the books of accounts  to  properly  classify  the  balances.
Further, compute and record the related  depreciation  expenses  to  reflect
the correct PPE book balances at year-end.

As a reiteration of the prior year’s audit recommendations,  we  recommended
to the Management the following courses of action:

1. Direct the Inventory/Property  Committee  to  conduct  an  effective  and
   complete physical inventory of all PPE  items  of  the  Municipality  and
   prepare the corresponding RPCPPE with remarks on  the  condition  of  the
   listed equipment.

2. Reconcile the results  thereof  with  the  available  accounting  records
   maintained by the Municipal Accounting  Office  to  determine  unrecorded
   properties and other  differences  for  further  validation.  Thereafter,
   submit a report on the results of  reconciliation  made  for  review  and
   verification.

3. Maintain PPE ledger cards and  property  cards  to  facilitate  efficient
   reconciliation of physical count versus accounting and property records.

4. Provide necessary adjusting entries in the books of  accounts  if  deemed
   necessary.

Recommendations:

We recommended to Management the following:

1. Require the MSWDO and Municipal  Accountant  to  submit  immediately  the
   complete Relief Distribution Sheets as proof that all procured items were
   actually received by the affected families and individuals;

2. Provide a written justification on the variances noted  in the number  of
   beneficiaries based on the RDS and on  the  unaccounted  food  items  for
   which receipt was not indicated in the RDS; and

3. Require the MSWDO to impose stricter  controls  in  the  distribution  of
   relief goods particularly in  the  documentation  by  ensuring  that  the
   required  information  in  the  RDS  are  completely  provided,  all  the
   recipients affixed their signature in the RDS as proof of receipt of  the
   food items, and the total number of recipients are  reconciled  with  the
   expected number of beneficiaries.

Recommendations:

We recommended to Management the following:

1. Require Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the  lacking  DVs  and
   supporting documents;

2. Provide a written justification on the deficiencies noted in the  conduct
   of procurement of fuel, oil and lubricants;

3. Require the BAC and end-users  to  comply  with  the  proper  procurement
   timeline in the purchase of fuel, oil and lubricants; and

4. Stop the practice of withdrawing fuel from suppliers without the  benefit
   of an approved contract or PO.

We recommended to Management the following courses of action:

1. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking DVs;

2. Require the BAC to provide an explanation for the price variance  in  the
   procurement of rice in the month of May 2021, and to submit the basis for
   the award of contract for transactions amounting to P537,250.00;

3. Direct the BAC to comply with the posting  requirements  as  required  in
   the GPPB Circular. For posting of the contract/PO at a conspicuous  place
   within agency  premises,  provide  a  bulletin  board  for  the  same  as
   requested by  the  BAC  Secretariat.  Further,  consider  the  hiring  of
   additional personnel to assist the BAC Secretariat to  ensure  compliance
   with the requirements of RA 9184 and other GPPB issuances;

4. Improve the agency’s procurement procedure by ensuring  that  the  budget
   data is anchored from reliable  sources  of  price  data  available  from
   implementing agencies (DA, DTI, DOH,  etc.)  or  from  actual  prevailing
   market prices; and

5. Indicate the brand and other specifications of the goods  in  the  PO  as
   prescribed in COA Circular 96-010.

We recommended  directing  the  OIC-  Municipal  Accountant  to  maintain  a
special account in the General Fund for the 20%  Development  Fund,  prepare
the quarterly and annual Fund  Receipt  and  Utilization  Report  for  Other
Special Accounts, and submit the FRUR to the Office of  the  Auditor  on  or
before the 15th day of the month after each quarter.

We recommended that the Municipal Disaster  Risk  Reduction  and  Management
Council (MDRRMC) follow the prescribed format of the  LDRMMFIP  as  provided
in Annex A of the aforementioned COA Circular, and  ensure  that  identified
PAPs and their expected outputs are specific and measurable to  aid  in  the
evaluation  of  program  implementation.  Likewise,  indicate  the  specific
timeline for the PPA implementation in the LDRRMFIP.

We recommended to the Management the following courses of action:

   1. Refrain from using the 5% LDRRMF for expenditure items  that  are  not
      directly related to disaster prevention and mitigation,  preparedness,
      response, and rehabilitation and recovery;

   2. Provide adequate  funding  for  the  regular  operating  expenses  and
      capital  outlay  of  the  MDRRMO,  particularly  for  the  repair  and
      maintenance of motor vehicles; and

   3. Direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the lacking  DVs
      and supporting documents related  to  LDRRMF  to  the  Office  of  the
      Auditor, to preclude disallowances/suspensions in the audit.

Recommendation:

We recommended that Management strictly adhere  to  the  guidelines  on  the
grant  and  liquidation  of  cash  advances  for  CF  specifically  on   the
submission of the disbursement vouchers  and  supporting  documents  to  the
Office of the Auditor within seven (7) days  after  release  of  the  check.
Further, stop the  practice  of  granting  cash  advance  to  the  SDO  with
unliquidated cash advances.

Recommendations:

We recommended to Management the following:

1. Provide a written justification on the deficiencies noted in the  conduct
   of procurement of fuel, oil and lubricants;

2. Require the BAC and end-users  to  comply  with  the  proper  procurement
   timeline in the purchase of fuel, oil and lubricants; and

3. Stop the practice of withdrawing fuel from suppliers without the  benefit
   of an approved contract or PO.

4. Require the drivers/end-users to completely  and  properly  fill  in  the
   required information in the Driver’s Trip Tickets; and

5. Direct the Municipal Accountant to review the completeness  and  accuracy
   of the  supporting  documents  for  fuel  consumption/utilization  before
   payment.

We recommended for the MDRRMO to judiciously  prepare  the  AIP  for  LDRRMF
incorporating  time  schedules,  availability   of   resources   and   other
contingencies to avoid delay in the implementation  of  PPAs.  Further,  the
MDRRMO is enjoined to improve the utilization rate of  LDRRMF  and  expedite
the implementation of identified PPAs, to strengthen the  agency’s  capacity
in mitigating and preparing for,  responding  to  and  recovering  from  the
impact of disasters.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Management the following courses of action:


   a. Enforce effective and efficient control measures to ensure timely turn-
      over of collections from  collectors  and  tellers  to  the  Municipal
      Treasurer. Further, direct the  Municipal  Treasurer  to  deposit  all
      collections intact and regularly to the AGDB to avoid the accumulation
      of undeposited collections at month-end.


   b. Review the  other  deficiencies  noted,  submit  the  noted  documents
      subject for audit review, and effect the necessary  adjusting  entries
      to correct the balances affecting Cash and  Cash  Equivalents  account
      and other related accounts.

We recommended to the Management the following courses of action:


     a. Direct the OIC - Municipal Accountant to ensure proper  preparation
        and  complete  submission  of  the  monthly   bank   reconciliation
        statements and  related  bank  statements  for  all  accounts  with
        outstanding balances per records. This is to ascertain  the  timely
        identification of errors and adjustments in  the  books.  Likewise,
        fast track the full validation of the identified reconciling  items
        for preparation of the necessary adjustments and journal  entry  to
        account the same  in  the  books.  This  is  to  establish  a  fair
        presentation of account balances in the financial statements.


     b. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to verify and confirm the status  of
        the remaining non-moving, dormant and closed bank accounts with its
        depository bank and make appropriate action  to  avoid  closure  of
        accounts.  After  verification,  coordinate  with  MACCO   in   the
        determination of the necessary adjusting entries in  the  books  to
        reflect the correct ending balance of the Cash in Bank account.

We recommended that OIC - Municipal Accountant trace  the  nature  of
balances reflected in the closed bank  accounts,  and  to  submit  to  this
office the results thereof together with certifications from  the  bank  on
the existence of the balances. An  evaluation  shall  be  made  on  whether
losses were  incurred  and  who  shall  be  responsible  for  such  losses.
Additionally, make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect  the  correct
balance of the Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account.

We recommended the following courses of action:


a. Enforce the  immediate  liquidation  of  the  outstanding  cash  advances
   through the issuance of letters of  demand  to  officials  and  employees
   concerned and strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular  97-002
   in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.


b. Conduct regular and periodic verification and analysis of  the  existence
   of receivables and unliquidated cash advances to determine the extent  of
   collectability.


c. Request for the write-off of dormant accounts and  submit  the  certified
   relevant documents validating the existence of the conditions.

As a reiteration of prior year’s audit recommendations, we recommended
to the Management the following courses of action:


     a. Direct the Inventory/Property Committee to conduct an effective and
        complete physical inventory of all PPE items  of  the  Municipality
        and prepare the corresponding RPCPPE with remarks on the  condition
        of the listed equipment.


     b. Reconcile the results thereof with the available accounting records
        maintained by the Municipal  Accounting  Office  to  determine  any
        unrecorded properties and other differences for further validation.
        Thereafter, submit a report on the results of  reconciliation  made
        for review and verification.


     c.  Maintain  PPE  ledger  cards  and  property  cards  to  facilitate
        efficient reconciliation of physical count  versus  accounting  and
        property records.


     d. Provide the necessary adjusting entries in the books of accounts if
        deemed necessary.

We recommended that MACCO review the  details  of  the  aforementioned
deficiencies, coordinate with the project implementers (MEO) for the  status
of various PPAs  and  provide  adjustments  in  the  books  of  accounts  to
properly classify the balances. Further,  compute  and  record  the  related
depreciation expenses to reflect the correct PPE book balances at  year-end.

We recommended that reclassification and adjusting entries be made  by
the OIC - Municipal Accountant in the General  Fund  books  to  reflect  the
correct PPE account balances as at  year-end.  Further,  the  management  is
enjoined to review the validity of capitalized small expense  items  as  PPE
in prior years.

We recommended that  MACCO  prioritize  the  analysis  of  the  ending
balance of Government Equity account under the  Trust  Fund  books,  and  to
determine and effect the  appropriate  adjustments  or  reclassification  of
accounts for a fair presentation of the financial statements.

We  recommended  that  management  shall  strictly  comply  with   the
provisions of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184, COA Circular 2009-001,  and  COA
Circular 2012-001, specifically on the following:


     a. BAC should prepare and submit APP to DBM and COA in accordance with
        the required timeline;


     b. Submit the received copies of invitation to observers  as  part  of
        the supporting documents to the approved contract agreement;


     c. Ensure that NOAs and NTPs are posted in the PhilGEPS;


     d. Submit the contracts/POs and its supporting documents  within  five
        (5) working days from the execution thereof; and


     e. Ensure the timely preparation and submission of the PMR to GPPB and
        copy furnish to COA.

We recommended for  the  Municipal  Mayor  the  following  courses  of
action:


     a. Direct the MDRRMO and MBO to judiciously plan and  incorporate  the
        required PPAs for LDRRMF utilization  in  the  AIP,  including  the
        expenditures chargeable under the special trust  fund  and  savings
        from  completed  CO  projects,  infrastructure  projects,  and  the
        unforeseen/unplanned expenditures.


     b. Direct the MDRRMO  to  review  the  processes  of  formulating  the
        supplemental AIP for LDRRMF, and ensure  better  coordination  with
        the OIC - Municipal Accountant and MBO in determining the available
        funds from unexpended balances of QRF and MOOE, donations  received
        for DRRM purposes, and  savings  from  completed  projects  of  the
        continuing appropriations.


     c. Direct the OIC - Municipal Accountant  to  immediately  submit  the
        copies of canceled checks and the unsubmitted disbursement vouchers
        and its supporting  documents  related  to  the  charges  in  Trust
        Liabilities – DRRMF account  to  the  Office  of  the  Auditor,  to
        preclude disallowances/suspensions in the audit.


Management’s Comments:

      The preparation of the Supplemental MDRRMFIP  for  the  special  trust
fund and continuing appropriations  for  the  LDRRMF  was  referred  to  the
Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal  Budget  Office.  Nonetheless,  in
the current year, the MDRRMC will ensure that there is  proper  coordination
between the MDRRMC and the Accounting and Budget Office to ensure  that  all
the  available  fund  sources  for  LDRRM  are  accounted  accordingly   and
incorporated in the MDRRMFIP.

Management’s Comments:

      The preparation of the Supplemental MDRRMFIP  for  the  special  trust
fund and continuing appropriations  for  the  LDRRMF  was  referred  to  the
Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal  Budget  Office.  Nonetheless,  in
the current year, the MDRRMC will ensure that there is  proper  coordination
between the MDRRMC and the Accounting and Budget Office to ensure  that  all
the  available  fund  sources  for  LDRRM  are  accounted  accordingly   and
incorporated in the MDRRMFIP.

We recommended to the Management the following courses of action:


   1. Utilize the LDRRMF and the unexpended prior years’ appropriations  for
      purposes of disaster risk and reduction  management  as  cited  in  RA
      10121 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1, and refrain from  using  the
      same for expenditure items that are not directly related with disaster
      prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, and  rehabilitation
      and recovery.


   2. Direct the OIC  -  Municipal  Accountant  to  immediately  submit  the
      lacking DVs and supporting documents related to LDRRMF to  the  Office
      of the Auditor, to preclude disallowances/suspensions in the audit.

We recommended that management discontinue the use of 20% DF  for  the
payment of administrative expenses to preclude the  issuance  of  suspension
or disallowance in the audit.

We recommended the following courses of action:


     1. Require the department heads/designated supervisors to prepare  the
        statement of duties and responsibilities  as  the  basis  for  work
        assignments and to ensure that actual accomplishments are  relevant
        to the requirements of the office.


     2. Require job orders to prepare  and  submit  accomplishment  reports
        that are specific in terms of actual  duties  performed  such that
        proper evaluation may be made on the necessity of hiring/re-hiring.

We recommended that management ensure that charges to the GAD fund are
consistent with the DILG approved GPB  and  in  accordance  with  the  joint
memorandum circular.

We recommended the following courses of action:


     1. Discontinue the payment of overtime services above what is  allowed
        as set forth under CSC and DBM Joint Circular No. 2 dated  November
        25, 2015;


     2. Discontinue the payment of overtime services for personnel who  are
        on travel status. Further, ensure that entries in the itinerary  of
        travels should coincide with those in the daily time records;


     3. Formulate and adopt internal rules and procedures on the  rendition
        of overtime services of LGU personnel which shall be  aligned  with
        the policies and guidelines under the same Joint Circular; and


     4. Submit a written justification for the  aforesaid  deficiencies  to
        enable us to decide on in the audit on whether to  suspend/disallow
        the transactions

We recommended to the Management the following courses of action:


     a. Strictly enforce the policy on “No Segregation, No  Collection”  at
        the waste source.


     b. Facilitate the immediate implementation of the strategies laid down
        in the ESWM Plan and the  acquisition  of  necessary  equipment  by
        improving manpower and appropriating sufficient  funds.  Also,  the
        Management is  reminded  to  prioritize  the  construction  of  the
        sanitary landfill as the final disposal site.


     c. Give emphasis during IEC on the  critical  role  of  barangays  and
        constituents in  the  full  implementation  of  the  ESWM  Plan  by
        offering incentives or imposing sanctions pursuant  to  Section  45
        and 50 of RA 9003.

We recommended to the MDRRMO to judiciously prepare the AIP for LDRRMF
incorporating  time  schedules,  availability   of   resources   and   other
contingencies to avoid delay in the implementation  of  PPAs.  Further,  the
MDRRMO is enjoined to improve the utilization rate of  LDRRMF  and  expedite
the implementation of identified PPAs, to strengthen the  agency’s  capacity
in mitigating and preparing for,  responding  to  and  recovering  from  the
impact of disasters.

We recommended  that  management  fast  track  the  implementation  of
developmental  projects  embodied  in  the  AIP  in  accordance   with   the
prescribed schedule of implementation to  accelerate  socio-economic  growth
and environmental development in the municipality.  We  further  recommended
that MPDO and MEO exercise diligently on  their  monitoring  functions  such
that issues and concerns that cause the delay in the project  implementation
could be immediately identified and  addressed.  Moreover,  the  program  of
works and necessary feasibility  study  should  be  prepared  and  conducted
before the project proposal so that projects identified will be  immediately
implemented.

We recommended that management judiciously monitor the  implementation
of projects funded by National Government Agencies. Further, follow up  with
the National Housing Authority the result on the request  for  an  amendment
to the MOA  particularly  on  the  release  of  additional  funds  based  on
progress billings and submit the necessary requirements  to  facilitate  the
subsequent release of the funds.

We recommended that management utilize the remaining fund from DA  for
the provision of electrical wirings and concrete flooring so that the  three
(3) cacao post-harvest facilities will become operational.

Further, request for the write-off of dormant accounts and submit the certified relevant documents validating the existence of the conditions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit immediately the monthly bank reconciliation statements for the period October to December 2017 and to prepare the necessary journal entry vouchers (JEVs) to record the reconciling items affecting the accounts.

We recommended the management to direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the accounts. Moreover, compute and record the related depreciation expense to reflect the correct PPE book balance at year end.

We recommended the management to enforce the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advances thru the issuance of letters of demand to officials and employees concerned and strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances. Further, request for the write-off of dormant accounts and submit the certified relevant documents validating the existence of the conditions.

We recommended the inventory/property committee to conduct an inventory of all the properties of the municipality and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) with the corresponding remarks on the condition of the listed equipment.

We recommended the management to designate a permanently appointed official/employee to perform disbursing functions. Further, ensure that the disbursing officer is adequately bonded and strictly adhere to the laws, rules and regulations in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances as required under Section 339 of R.A. 7160 and Section 4.1.4 of COA Circular 97-002.

We recommended the management to direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to immediately cause the closure of the dormant deposit account with PNB and transfer the account balances, if there is any, to an active account maintained in an Authorized Government Depository Bank. Make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct cash in bank balance at year end.

We recommended the management to direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to trace the JEV of the initial deposit. Further, if found that it was actually credited as part of the loans payable, make necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the accounts.

We recommended the management to effect adjusting journal entries in order to reflect the correct account balance. Further, adopt an effective set of internal control/s on recording the transactions relating to liability accounts.

We recommended the management to refrain from using LDRRMF for programs, projects and activities which are not directly related with disaster preparedness, mitigation, and response and rescue operations. Further, strictly adhere to the provisions of existing rules and regulations in the proper utilization of the fund.

We recommended that management should prioritize the  hiring  of four (4) required permanent  MDRRMO personnel who will be exclusively performing  disaster risk reduction and management related functions.

We recommended that the LDRRMO should prepare monthly the Report of Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to be certified by the Municipal Accountant and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor through the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (LDRRMC) and Local Development Council (LDC) on or before 15th day of the following month.

We recommended that the MDRRMO  should prepare the LDRRMFIP in a standard format required by COA Circular 2012-002. Also, incorporate all programs, projects and activities in the LDRRMFIP  as required including the expenditures chargeable under the special trust fund, infrastructure projects of the LDRRMF and the unforeseen/unplanned expenditures.

We recommended the management to direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant, Municipal  Budget Officer and Municipal Treasurer to coordinate in updating the utilization of LDRRMF in order to determine and transfer immediately the unexpended/unobligated balances of QRF and DRRMF-MOOE from the General Fund books to Trust Fund books on or before the last banking day of the year.

We recommended the management to adopt a proper and effective inventory of supplies and materials management system. Further, the Municipal Accountant should ensure that all procurements were properly recorded using the appropriate accounts.

We recommended that management fast-track the safe closure of the dump site and to prioritize the establishment of sanitary landfill as final disposal site for solid wastes. Operation and design of this landfill shall be in accordance with guidelines mentioned under R.A. 9003.

We recommended  the  management to  direct the Municipal Treasurer to exert more effort in the collection of real property taxes so that the PPAs identified by the local school board will be fully implemented and maximized. Further, utilize funds in accordance with its intended purpose.
We recommended the Municipal Accountant to carefully examine and ensure the correctness and completeness of supporting documents prior to payment.
We recommended the management to ensure that funds are utilized in accordance with its intended purpose and fast track the implementation of the PPAs during the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer should provide the correct list of RPT due during the year and delinquent taxes in prior  years as basis for the adjustments in the RPT and SET receivable accounts to reflect the correct balances as at year-end.

We recommended the management to submit a written justification for the failure to submit for post-audit the DVs and supporting documents on cash advances granted for CF. Further, strictly adhere to the guidelines on the grant and liquidation of cash advances for CF

We recommended the management to direct the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the aforementioned paid DVs to preclude the issuance of Notice of Suspensions/Disallowances for these transactions.

We recommend the management to direct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately transfer the funds received from DILG to the Trust Fund bank account and OIC-Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments.  Further, exert effort in tracing the long overdue accounts amounting to P1,074,313.00.

We recommended the management to  direct the Municipal Agriculturist/BUB focal person and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to immediately initiate the re-bidding at the soonest possible time to fast-track the implementation of the project.

We recommended the management to provide proper and sufficient trainings to members of the recipient PO so that they will be fully equipped on how to use the government funded projects. Further, conduct regular monitoring of the activities of the PO to ensure that the equipment and facilities are utilized according to its purpose. Moreover, evaluate the capacity of the CSO to sustain the program and if found to be not capable as in the case of the Boston Business Chamber, initiate measures such as identifying other accredited CSOs that has the capability to implement the same project.

We recommended the MAO to fast-track the formulation of operational plan for approval by BFAR so that operations of the CFLC may commence at the soonest possible time. Further, provide proper and sufficient trainings to capacitate the member of the recipient PO.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer initiate measures to enforce the collection on its RPT/SEF receivable such as imposition of legal remedies. Likewise, assign additional personnel to monitor the collection on RPT/SEF receivable and to ensure that all Notices of Delinquency are issued and received by taxpayers.

Direct the Municipal Agriculture Officer/BUB Focal Person to immediately forward the Purchase Requests of the materials and equipment to be procured to the Bids and Awards Committee.  Upon submission, require the Bids and Awards Committee to expedite the procurement process.
observations.reason: The project “Acquisition of 4 Units High Value Crop Development Program (Cacao Post Harvest Equipment Facilities)” had remained unimplemented since it was overlooked by the BUB focal person due to voluminous projects that need to be monitored. Further, the specifications thereof were not clearly stated in the project proposal and memorandum of agreement which eventually caused difficulty during procurement on the part of the MAO. Reiterated in AAR 2017
